chunk_summary: Tele-nutrition for children with neurodisability focuses on aspiration risk assessment and caregiver burden management using structured checklists and feeding safety strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of assessing aspiration risk in children with neurodisability during tele-nutrition consultations.
Learn how to evaluate caregiver burden and its impact on feeding plans.
Identify feeding safety screening criteria and strategies for improving meal efficiency.
Apply decision algorithms to balance feeding safety and nutritional needs.
key_facts: Aspiration risk assessment should precede calorie escalation in tele-nutrition for neurodisability.
Feeding safety screening includes coughing, choking, wet voice, recurrent chest infections, and prolonged feeding times.
Caregiver burden is measured by meal duration, daily feeding hours, and ability to sustain feeding plans.
Unsafe textures should be paused, and in-person swallow evaluations arranged if aspiration risk is suspected.
Tele-nutrition can focus on energy density and meal efficiency if feeding safety is stable.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Children with neurodisability often have feeding safety issues and high caregiver burden, so tele-nutrition must prioritize aspiration risk assessment before calorie escalation. (Source: ESPGHAN position paper)
document_key_topics: Feeding safety screening and decision algorithms
